category_title: Preschoolers who throw tantrums during clean-up transitions
minimal_context: Olivia is in the classroom dramatic play center and is asked to clean up her activities.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Olivia, it is time to clean up the dramatic play center.

STUDENT: No! I want to keep playing!

TUTOR: You need to put the toys away now.

STUDENT: (Starts crying loudly) I don't want to clean up!

TUTOR: Please start picking up the items.

STUDENT: (Drops to the floor sobbing and screaming)

options.A: Review a daily behavior sheet, have the student count the positive and negative marks, and discuss whether she had an overall positive or negative day.
options.B: Collaboratively identify social-emotional targeted goals with clearly defined definitions of desirable and undesirable behaviors.
options.C: Model complete sentences as often as possible during dramatic play and clean-up transitions.
options.D: Provide teacher-controlled activity breaks on a scheduled time frame, making them available to the student for free rather than contingent upon clean-up performance.
